plugin_desc: EtherWAN EX-series industrial managed switch-specific SNMP collection (IANA PEN 2736). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects EtherWAN private OIDs for device health — chassis temperature (ewnSensorTempValue, a dedicated scalar whose raw value divided by 10 gives degrees Celsius) and the dual power-input status table (ewnPowerSupplyTable ewnPowerSupplyStatus, one row per power input named Power1/Power2). The power raw codes (0=up meaning OK, 1=down meaning input lost or absent) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault, and the worst power input is taken per device. EtherWAN EX-series devices are fanless and expose no CPU, memory or fan OIDs, so those are not collected (N/A). Supported brands/models — EtherWAN EX-series hardened industrial Ethernet switches (EX63000/EX73000 etc.).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the EtherWAN device (private ewnSensorTempValue scalar under IANA PEN 2736). The raw object value is in tenths of a degree, so the query divides it by 10 to obtain Celsius. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction in the cabinet and risk thermal damage. The temperature sensor is SKU-dependent; on models without a sensor this object is absent and the metric is simply not reported.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply health of the EtherWAN device, derived from the dual power-input status table (ewnPowerSupplyTable ewnPowerSupplyStatus, one row per input). The raw codes (0=up meaning the input is present and OK, 1=down meaning the input is lost or absent) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and 2 = fault, and the worst input is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a failed or absent power input that warrants investigation.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
